claim_results.claim: Each dead branch shows exactly why it dies.
::: ::: desc The claim destroys itself when stated. ::: :::::: :::::: method-card ::: label
::: ::: desc Claim contradicts what we observe. ::: :::::: :::::: method-card ::: label
::: ::: branch-answer No, nothing exists. ::: ::: branch-reason [SELF-REFUTATION]{.death-type .self-refute} The denial requires a denier.
To say \"nothing exists\" is to instantiate a claim, which is something.
::: ::: branch-answer The question is meaningless. ::: ::: branch-reason [SELF-REFUTATION]{.death-type .self-refute} Declaring meaninglessness requires a meaning-bearing entity to declare it.
::: ::: branch-answer Existence is an illusion. ::: ::: branch-reason [COLLAPSES → Q0-B]{.death-type .circular} An illusion requires something to be illusory *for*.

If \"something\" exists, the word implies it is not \"nothing.\" But is the distinction real? ::: :::::: :::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::: branches :::::::::: {.branch .dead} ::::: branch-status ::: status-icon ✕ ::: ::: status-label
But stating THIS requires distinguishing the two levels --- recursion.
Carries cost forward. ::: :::::: :::::::::: ::::: system-map ::: {.sys-badge .theophysics} SYS-B: A1.2 --- \"Existence requires distinguishability.\" ::: ::: {.sys-badge .secular} SYS-A: Agrees.
::: ::: branch-answer Another structure grounds it --- turtles all the way down. ::: ::: branch-reason [INFINITE REGRESS]{.death-type .regress} Every \"ground\" requires a deeper ground.

::: ::: branch-answer A multiverse generates all possible substrates --- anthropic selection. ::: ::: branch-reason [UNFALSIFIABLE + REGRESS]{.death-type .explanatory} (a) No observable test distinguishes this from Q4-A. (b) What grounds the multiverse mechanism?
::: ::: branch-answer Information requires observation to become actual. ::: ::: branch-reason **Participatory universe.** Wheeler\'s delayed-choice.
Potential requires actualization, actualization requires observer.
::: ::: branch-answer No --- sign change requires external operator (grace). ::: ::: branch-reason **The system cannot flip itself.** An external, non-unitary operator (Ĝ) is required.
::: ::: branch-answer Trajectories oscillate forever --- no asymptote. ::: ::: branch-reason [ENERGY PROBLEM]{.death-type .circular} Requires perpetual energy input to prevent settling.
Intellectual honesty requires naming them. ::::::::::::::::::: blanks-grid :::::::::: {.blanks-col .secular-blanks}
